title: Blob Description
description:
Blob Description

Blob Description

Blob objects compute geometric, topological and other features of images.  Blob objects are useful for determining presence/absence, size and orientation of features in an image.  For example, Blob objects can be used for detecting the presence, size and location of ink dots on silicon wafers, for determining the orientation of a component, or even for robot guidance.  (However, it is recommended to use the Polar object for the decision of the rotation direction.)

Some of the features computed by Blob objects are:

Area and perimeter
Center of mass
Principal axes and moments of inertia
Connectivity
Extrema
Coordinate positions of the center of mass in pixel, camera coodinate system, and robot coordinate system
Holes, roughness, and compactness of blobs
